categories.name: Improper Credential Usage
categories.description: Hardcoded credentials, insecure credential storage, and improper handling of authentication tokens. Credentials can be extracted through reverse engineering or device access.

categories.name: Inadequate Supply Chain Security
categories.description: Risks from third-party libraries and SDKs without proper vetting, integrity verification, or version pinning. Compromised dependencies can introduce malware or backdoors.

categories.name: Insecure Authentication/Authorization
categories.description: Weaknesses in authentication including biometric flaws, missing MFA, and client-side authorization checks that can be bypassed.

categories.name: Insufficient Input/Output Validation
categories.description: Lack of input validation on deep links, IPC mechanisms, clipboard, and other input channels enabling injection and path traversal attacks.

categories.name: Insecure Communication
categories.description: Missing certificate pinning, disabled TLS verification, cleartext traffic, and improper hostname validation enabling MITM attacks.

categories.name: Inadequate Privacy Controls
categories.description: Excessive collection of personal data, device identifiers, and location without consent. Includes lack of data minimization and missing consent mechanisms.

categories.name: Insufficient Binary Protections
categories.description: Absence of code obfuscation, anti-tampering, root/jailbreak detection, and debugger detection allowing reverse engineering.

categories.name: Security Misconfiguration
categories.description: Insecure defaults: debug mode in production, enabled backups, cleartext traffic, exported components without protection.

categories.name: Insecure Data Storage
categories.description: Sensitive data in unprotected locations: external storage, unencrypted databases, cache, logs, clipboard. Accessible by other apps or extractable from device.

categories.name: Insufficient Cryptography
categories.description: Weak or deprecated algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, ECB), hardcoded encryption keys, insufficient key lengths, improper crypto implementation.
